tldr: Move from Braze's all-in-one engagement suite to Courier's notification routing API with template management across channels.
why_switch: Lower entry cost vs. Braze enterprise contracts
Simpler notification API surface focused on routing
Channel abstraction (push, email, SMS, Slack, in-app) behind one template
Less platform lock-in around segmentation and journeys
Freemium tier on Courier vs. paid-only Braze
export_method: Use Braze's REST API and the user export endpoints to pull profiles, attributes, and event history; export templates and Canvas definitions through the Braze dashboard or API where supported. For data not exposed via API (e.g., some campaign analytics), request a CSV export through Braze support or use scheduled S3 exports if your plan includes Currents. Verify tokens (push, email subscription state) are included before disconnecting.
import_method: Provision a Courier workspace, configure providers (FCM/APNs, your ESP, SMS) under Channels, then upload or recreate templates in Courier's template editor. Push user data via the Courier API (profile/preferences endpoints) and adapt your application code to call Courier's send API instead of Braze's. Re-implement journey logic using Courier's Automations or your own orchestration layer. Consult official Courier docs for current endpoint shapes.

concept_mapping.note: Maps via external_id; verify attribute naming.
Feature parity is partial — re-design flows, do not assume 1:1.
Courier is more API-driven; scheduled blasts may need an external scheduler.
Confirm current Courier audience capabilities in docs.

gotchas: Push token format and provider credentials must be re-uploaded; tokens are tied to bundle IDs.
Liquid vs. Courier template syntax differences will break personalization if copy-pasted.
Journey re-implementation rarely maps 1:1 — budget design time.
Analytics history does not transfer; keep a Braze read-only seat during transition.
Run both platforms in parallel for one full lifecycle window before cutover.

email.subject: Migrating from Braze to Courier
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning a migration from Braze to Courier for our messaging stack. The goal is a simpler, API-first notification layer with lower fixed cost.

High-level plan: export user profiles, attributes, push tokens, templates, and segment definitions from Braze; recreate templates and re-route sends through Courier; re-implement journeys as Courier Automations or in our own orchestration. We'll run both systems in parallel for a lifecycle window before cutting over.

Estimated effort: 3-6 weeks. Main risks: template syntax differences, journey re-design, and analytics history not transferring.

Let me know if you want to review the cutover checklist.
